calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-10-2022 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2022 Continued To Aug-23-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar May-25-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ALAN ZIMMERMAN and MONICA ZIMMERMAN'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

Defendants' (Alan P. Zimmerman [Alan] and Monica K. Zimmerman [Monica], individually and doing business as Mark Adam & Co.) motions to quash are granted.

When a defendant challenges jurisdiction by bringing a motion to quash, the burden is on the plaintiff to prove the existence of jurisdiction by proving, inter alia, the facts requisite to an effective service. (See Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1439-1440.) Plaintiff must present competent evidence to maintain his burden and a declaration that is not signed under penalty of perjury, as required by CCP Sec. 2015.5, is inadmissible. (See Van Sickle v Gilbert (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1495, 1510, n10.)

Here, the only purported evidence to demonstrate service are the proofs of service that were filed with the court on April 8, 2022.

The Monica proof of service, which contends personal service, is not signed in compliance with CCP Sec. 2015.5 because it was not executed under penalty of perjury. Pursuant to the judicial council form, the server stated that he is a California sheriff or marshal and certified that the foregoing is true and correct. Despite the form's indication that this alternative certification is proper, the court is unaware of and has not been directed to any
authority that exempts sheriffs and marshals from the penalty of perjury attestation requirement.

The Alan proof of service presents a closer call but suffers from the same infirmity. The Alan proof of service claims substitute service. While the diligence and mailing portions of the proof of service are under penalty of perjury, the portion of the declaration that claims that the summons and complaint were left at Alan's business is not under penalty of perjury. Plaintiff therefore fails to demonstrate proper service on the defendants.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

Motion For Order That Settlement Agreement Is Enforceable And Binding, And Request For Attorneys Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Janine R. Menhennet
Off Calendar May-25-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SCOTT BATCHELOR'S Motion For Order That Settlement Agreement Is Enforceable And Binding, And Request For Attorneys Fees.

Plaintiff's "Motion for Order that Settlement Agreement is Enforceable and Binding, and Request for Attorneys Fees" is denied without prejudice. First, plaintiff has not demonstrated proper service of the summons and complaint on the defendants. Therefore, the court lacks jurisdiction to entertain this motion. Second, plaintiff seeks "an order enforcing a written settlement agreement with all defendants." (Notice of Motion 1:25-26.) Plaintiff is not seeking entry of a judgment. CCP Sec. 664.6(a) provides: "If parties to pending litigation stipulate, in a writing signed by the parties outside of the presence of the court or orally before the court, for settlement of the case, or part thereof, the court, upon motion, may enter judgment pursuant to the terms of the settlement. If requested by the parties, the court may retain jurisdiction over the parties to enforce the settlement until performance in full of the terms of the settlement."

A request for the trial court to retain jurisdiction under section 664.6 "must conform to the same three requirements which the Legislature and the courts have deemed necessary for section 664.6 enforcement of the settlement itself: the request must be made (1) during the pendency of the case, not after the case has been dismissed in its entirety, (2) by the parties themselves, and (3) either in a writing signed by the parties or orally before the court." (Wackeen v. Malis (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 429, 440; see also Sayta v. Chu (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 960 [trial court lacked jurisdiction to enforce settlement because parties did not request that court retain jurisdiction].) The "request must be express, not implied from other language, and it must be clear and unambiguous." (Wackeen, 97 Cal.App.4th at 440.) Here, there is no evidence showing that the parties made the required request for the court to retain jurisdiction.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
